 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.1","title":"VFX perf harness: deterministic run + effect lock","description":"Background\n- Visual Effects perf profiling is noisy because AppModel initializes all screens and effects on startup. We need a deterministic, VFX-only harness to measure real frame cost without unrelated initialization overhead.\n\nGoals\n- Provide a repeatable harness that locks to a single VFX effect, fixed terminal size, fixed tick cadence, and fixed duration/frames.\n- Ensure the harness avoids initializing non‑VFX screens entirely (fast-path or VFX-only AppModel path).\n\nScope / Work Items\n- Add or extend CLI/env options (FTUI_DEMO_* family) to force: screen=VisualEffects, effect=<name>, fixed cols/rows, fixed tick cadence (Hz or ms), and auto-exit after N frames or N ms.\n- Implement a VFX-only startup path that bypasses heavy ScreenStates::default and constructs only VisualEffects state (plus minimal runtime plumbing).\n- Provide a single documented command line for deterministic profiling and for CI/e2e usage.\n- Emit structured JSONL logs for harness runs: init metadata, effect name, size, frame index, hash, timing, and seed/time control.\n\nConstraints\n- No visual quality/resolution reduction and no algorithmic shortcuts that alter output.\n- Deterministic output for identical inputs (seed/time, tick cadence, size).\n- Prefer existing CLI/config surfaces; avoid new files unless absolutely necessary.\n\nTesting + Logging\n- Add/extend an e2e test that runs the harness twice and asserts identical hash sequences.\n- JSONL logging must include: run_id, effect, size, tick_ms, frame_idx, hash, seed/time.\n- Provide clear failure diagnostics (first mismatched frame, expected vs actual hash).\n\nNotes\n- This harness is a prerequisite for meaningful profiling and later optimization.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Single-line deterministic harness command exists and is documented in code comments/tests.\n2) Harness avoids initializing non‑VFX screens (verified via logs or a test).\n3) Two runs with identical inputs produce identical per‑frame hashes.\n4) JSONL logs include all required fields and are schema‑valid.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:56:53.029445192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:10:48.205149356Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:10:48.205130971Z","close_reason":"Completed (VFX harness + deterministic hashes + JSONL logging)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:56:53.029445192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.10","title":"VFX perf regression guard + CI evidence logs","description":"Background
- We need a regression guard so VFX perf gains remain stable and detectable in CI.
- The guard should be deterministic, hash-verified, and report clear diagnostics.

Goals
- Add a VFX perf regression gate using the harness + timing logs.
- Fail fast on significant regressions with actionable JSONL diagnostics.

Scope / Work Items
- Define baseline thresholds per effect/size from bd-3e1t.5.7 (p50/p95/p99 total_ms).
- Add an automated check (test or E2E script) that runs the harness at fixed seed/time and compares metrics against thresholds.
- Validate JSONL schema and include first-mismatch diagnostics (effect, size, frame_idx, expected vs actual hash, timing deltas).
- Ensure the guard is deterministic and does not introduce runtime overhead when disabled.

Constraints
- No visual quality or resolution changes.
- Use existing harness/logging; avoid new files unless required by test infra.

Tests / Validation
- Guard fails on synthetic regression and passes on baseline.
- JSONL logs are schema-stable and include perf + hash fields.

Dependencies
- Requires timing instrumentation (bd-3e1t.5.2), golden hashes (bd-3e1t.5.5), and baseline pack (bd-3e1t.5.7).","acceptance_criteria":"1) Automated guard compares p50/p95/p99 against baseline thresholds for defined effects and sizes.\n2) Guard fails with actionable diagnostics (effect, size, frame_idx, expected vs actual hash, timing deltas).\n3) JSONL schema validation passes and hashes remain stable for baseline runs.","notes":"Added PTY perf regression guard in crates/ftui-demo-showcase/tests/visual_effects_pty.rs. New test pty_vfx_perf_regression_guard runs 5 effects x 2 sizes (80x24, 120x40) at 120 frames, tick_ms=16, seed=0, parses vfx_perf_summary, and enforces p50/p95/p99 budgets from bd-3e1t.5.7 with 2x CI multiplier. Emits JSONL event vfx_perf_guard with actuals + budgets + deltas. Release-only (skips debug). Added synthetic regression unit test vfx_perf_budget_rejects_regression to validate budget logic. Hash guard: loads golden checksums if present and compares prefix, logging vfx_perf_guard_hash (outcome pass/fail/missing) and mismatch diagnostics; missing goldens are logged but do not fail. Validated: cargo fmt, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test -p ftui-demo-showcase --test visual_effects_pty pty_vfx_perf_regression_guard --release (pass).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:18.227923955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T01:06:40.889997103Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T01:06:40.889977215Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:18.227923955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:30.689758919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:34.664372423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:39.134892573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.12","title":"perf(PlasmaFx): apply sin/cos decomposition to cell-space render_with_palette","description":"Goal
Apply the canvas adapter's proven sin/cos precomputation approach to the PlasmaFx cell-space render path (render_with_palette), eliminating per-pixel sin/cos calls.

Current State
PlasmaFx::render_with_palette (plasma.rs:646-708) has 3-4 sin/cos calls per pixel in the inner loop:
- v3: sin((x + y) * 1.2 + time_0_6) — 1 sin/pixel
- v6: sin(sin_x2[dx] * cos_y2 + time_0_5) — 1 sin/pixel (Reduced+Full)
- v4: sin(radial_center_phase[idx] - time_1_2) — 1 sin/pixel (Full only)
- v5: cos(radial_offset_phase[idx] + time) — 1 cos/pixel (Full only)

PlasmaCanvasAdapter (canvas_adapters.rs) already eliminated all inner-loop trig using sin(a+b) decomposition with precomputed sin/cos bases — zero trig calls per pixel.

Approach
1. Extend PlasmaScratch with sin/cos base arrays for each wave component
2. Precompute sin/cos bases in ensure_geometry (geometry-only, once per resize)
3. In render_with_palette, compute per-frame time sin/cos outside the loop
4. Replace inner-loop sin/cos with multiply-add using precomputed bases
5. Hoist quality branching outside the inner loop (match on quality, then loop)

Constraints
- PlasmaFx output will change at floating-point rounding level (acceptable — no golden hashes for this path)
- Must pass all existing PlasmaFx tests
- Must pass cargo check/clippy/fmt/test
- One lever: sin/cos decomposition only, no other changes

Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/plasma.rs (PlasmaScratch + render_with_palette)

Validation
- cargo bench -p ftui-extras --bench plasma_bench --features visual-fx (measure improvement)
- cargo test -p ftui-extras --features visual-fx plasma (pass all tests)
- cargo check/clippy/fmt","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureDesert","created_at":"2026-02-09T13:26:43.183834156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:39:16.302282861Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:39:16.302262913Z","close_reason":"Applied sin/cos decomposition to PlasmaFx cell-space render_with_palette. All trig precomputed at geometry time; inner loops use multiply-add only. 120x40 Full: 97µs (5.2x under 500µs budget). Throughput ~49 Melem/s. Commit 5cfff7e5.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.12","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T13:26:43.183834156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.2","title":"Render-thread profiling + in-app timing for VFX","description":"Background
- We need precise, low-noise timing data for Visual Effects to drive real optimization.
- Flamegraphs alone don’t connect hotspots to frame phases; we need structured, in-app timing.

Goals
- Measure per-frame time spent in update, render, diff, and present for VFX runs.
- Keep instrumentation deterministic and behind a flag so normal demos remain clean.
- Produce JSONL logs that correlate with the VFX harness and golden hashes.

Scope / Work Items
- Add a perf timing toggle (env/CLI) used by the VFX harness.
- Instrument the VFX update/render pipeline with lightweight timers (no allocations in hot path).
- Emit per-frame JSONL events with: run_id, effect, size, tick_ms, frame_idx, update_ms, render_ms, diff_ms, present_ms, total_ms.
- Emit a summary event at end of run: count, p50/p95/p99 for total_ms and top phases.
- Ensure timers are monotonic and stable (avoid wall clock; prefer std::time::Instant).

Constraints
- No visual output changes, no quality/resolution reductions.
- When perf toggle is OFF, no extra logging or measurable overhead on hot path.
- Deterministic: log values must match frame order and inputs.

Tests / Validation
- Unit test for the summary aggregator (p50/p95/p99 on a fixed dataset).
- E2E test: run harness with perf enabled and validate JSONL schema + required fields.

- New QuakeFramebuffer::clear_depth() method for targeted z-buffer reset

- Hoisted loop-invariant computations from per-column loop: ceil_h, floor_h, pitch_offset, sx_span, vx_span, back_ceil, back_floor, has_upper, has_lower

- Hoisted per-triangle edge deltas (e12_dy/dx, e20_dy/dx) and per-row fy-dependent terms from rasterizer inner loop
- New QuakeFramebuffer::clear_depth() method for targeted z-buffer reset

**Also fixed pre-existing clippy issues:** wad.rs unnecessary_cast, framebuffer.rs identity_op

All changes preserve exact FP output (determinism/hash parity maintained).  {"id":"bd-4r5h","title":"Text Effects: Preset Library","description":"## Background
Users want great results without understanding individual effects. Presets provide curated, ready-to-use combinations that showcase the system's capabilities.

## Implementation

Add TextPreset enum:
```rust
pub enum TextPreset {
    // High-energy/modern
    Neon,       // Cyan/magenta wave, pulsing glow
    Cyberpunk,  // Glitch, chromatic aberration, animated gradient
    Matrix,     // Green cascade, fade trail, cyber chars
    
    // Classic/retro
    Retro,      // CRT scanlines, green phosphor, subtle flicker
    Typewriter, // Reveal by char with cursor block
    Terminal,   // Solid green, subtle pulse, no frills
    
    // Elegant/professional
    Cinematic,  // Slow fade, gold gradient, soft glow
    Elegant,    // Organic pulse, gold/cream gradient, drop shadow
    Minimal,    // Subtle fade, no color, clean
    
    // Dynamic/playful
    Fire,       // Fire gradient, upward wave motion
    Ice,        // Frost blue gradient, sparkle effect
    Hologram,   // Rainbow shift, scanlines, transparency pulse
}
```

### Preset Specifications

| Preset | Effects | Colors | Special |
|--------|---------|--------|---------|
| Neon | Wave, PulsingGlow, ColorWave | Cyan/Magenta | Bold text |
| Cyberpunk | Glitch, ChromaticAberration, AnimatedGradient | Cyberpunk palette | Occasional matrix chars |
| Matrix | Cascade, FadeOut trail, Glitch | Green shades | CyberChars |
| Retro | Scanline, ColorCycle, Glitch(0.02) | Terminal green | Phosphor glow |
| Typewriter | Reveal(LeftToRight), cursor | White | Monospace feel |
| Terminal | Pulse(subtle) | Green | Simple |
| Cinematic | FadeIn(eased), HorizontalGradient, Glow | Gold/White | Drop shadow |
| Elegant | OrganicPulse, Gradient, Shadow | Gold/Cream | Italic option |
| Minimal | FadeIn | Theme default | No decoration |
| Fire | AnimatedGradient(fire), Wave(up) | Fire palette | Bold |
| Ice | Gradient(ice), Sparkle, Pulse | Blue/White | - |
| Hologram | RainbowGradient, Scanline, Pulse | Rainbow | Transparency |

### Builder API:
```rust
// Apply preset
StyledText::preset(TextPreset::Neon)

// Customize after preset
StyledText::preset(TextPreset::Neon)
    .base_color(custom_cyan)  // Override default
    .easing(Easing::Bounce)   // Change animation feel

// Get preset effects for inspection
let effects = TextPreset::Neon.effects();
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_preset_applies_effects: Each preset sets non-empty effect list
- test_preset_customizable: base_color override works
- test_preset_easing_override: Can change easing after preset
- test_all_presets_render: No panic for any preset at t=0, t=0.5, t=1
- test_preset_effects_consistent: Same preset = same effects
- test_preset_no_conflicts: No preset has conflicting effects

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot all 12 presets at t=0.5
- Verify each preset is visually distinct

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Preset Library
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-6e9","title":"Bubbles Widget Collection (charmed_rust port)","description":"# Bubbles Widget Collection (optional, feature-gated)

## Purpose
This epic tracks additional widgets inspired by `charmed_rust` (Bubbletea/Bubbles ecosystem).

These are **not required** for the core ftui kernel or the first agent harness deliverable, but we keep them as optional feature-gated extensions.

## Reference Source
- `legacy_reference_library_code/charmed_rust/`

## Plan Alignment
The plan explicitly de-scopes “widget zoo” into Phase 7:
- ship kernel + runtime + harness first
- only then add extra widgets

## Scope
Widgets here must:
- render deterministically
- respect focus/input conventions
- avoid terminal side effects (all output goes through ftui’s one-writer routing)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Widgets are feature-gated and cannot affect kernel invariants when disabled.
- [ ] Each widget has snapshot tests + interaction/unit tests where applicable.
- [ ] Widgets integrate with ftui-style/theme and ftui-runtime subscriptions cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:57:47.683736546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:01:40.867306738Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:01:40.867228872Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6e9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":485,"issue_id":"bd-6e9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6e9.1","title":"Implement TextArea widget (multi-line text editor)","description":"# Implement TextArea widget (multi-line text editor)

## Objective
Implement a multi-line text editor widget suitable for agent harnesses and future tools.

This is **not required** for the v1 harness (which needs TextInput), but it’s a valuable Phase 7 extra.

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/charmed_rust/crates/bubbles/src/textarea.rs`

## Scope
- Editing:
  - insert text (including multi-line paste)
  - backspace/delete
  - newline
  - selection (optional v1; can be phased)
  - undo/redo (optional; can defer)
- Navigation:
  - arrows, home/end
  - word-left/word-right
  - page up/down (optional)
- View:
  - scrolling viewport within the editor
  - cursor visibility and scroll-to-cursor behavior
  - soft-wrap vs hard-wrap policy (explicit)

## Integration Notes
- Reuse text infrastructure rather than inventing a parallel one:
  - Rope storage (`bd-3ky.5`)
  - Editor ops (`bd-3ky.6`)
  - Cursor utilities (`bd-6e9.7`)
  - Grapheme segmentation helpers (`bd-6e9.8`)
- Rendering must be deterministic into `Buffer`/`Frame`.
- All input must come through ftui Event/InputParser.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for editor ops (insert/delete/move) with tricky Unicode.
- Snapshot tests for rendering under:
  - narrow widths
  - long lines
  - multi-line content
  - cursor at boundaries
- PTY test (optional) demonstrating interactive editing without terminal corruption.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TextArea supports basic multi-line editing and cursor navigation.
- [ ] Cursor never splits graphemes; visual column math matches ftui width rules.
- [ ] Rendering is stable and scroll-to-cursor works.
- [ ] Unit + snapshot tests cover representative and adversarial cases.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Multi-line text insertion and deletion works correctly
- [ ] Cursor movement: left/right/up/down/home/end/word navigation
- [ ] Text selection with Shift+Arrow, Ctrl+Shift+Arrow, Shift+Home/End
- [ ] Word wrapping: soft wrap preserves buffer, hard wrap inserts newlines
- [ ] Viewport scrolling follows cursor and supports manual scroll
- [ ] Undo/redo: all operations reversible, undo groups by word boundaries
- [ ] Clipboard: copy/cut/paste including bracketed paste support
- [ ] Line numbers display option
- [ ] Placeholder text shown when empty
- [ ] Character limit enforcement prevents exceeding limit
- [ ] Validation callback can reject invalid input

### Unicode Requirements
- [ ] ZWJ sequences (emoji families) treated as single grapheme
- [ ] Wide characters (CJK) render as 2 cells
- [ ] Combining characters handled correctly
- [ ] RTL text renders correctly (if bidi enabled)

### Performance Requirements
- [ ] Typing latency < 5ms for buffers up to 100K characters
- [ ] Scrolling latency < 16ms (60 FPS capable)
- [ ] Memory: < 3x document size overhead

### Logging Requirements
- [ ] tracing::debug! for cursor position changes
- [ ] tracing::debug! for selection changes
- [ ] tracing::trace! for individual character insertions
- [ ] tracing::info! for undo/redo operations
- [ ] tracing::warn! for validation rejections

### Test Coverage
- [ ] Unit tests for all cursor movement functions
